summary.intent: Build and fix browser viewing/interaction features in the moltis project, including a UI to view/control browser sessions created by the AI agent (Rex).
summary.outcome: Fixed multiple bugs (null serde deserialization, shared BrowserManager, screencast relay race condition, missing WebSocket subscription) and added new features (New Session button with loading state, URL autocomplete with Google suggestions, sandbox mode for UI-created sessions, scroll/interaction improvements, e2e tests).

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService previously had separate BrowserManager instances; they now share one via Arc<OnceCell> wired in server.rs
The v4 WebSocket protocol requires explicit event subscriptions; browser.screencast.frame was missing from the subscription list in websocket.js
The broadcast filter in broadcast.rs line 100-102 silently drops events for clients that haven't subscribed — no error, no log
sandbox: true must be explicitly injected for UI-initiated browser actions; the service defaults to host mode (sandbox.unwrap_or(false))
validate_url() in manager.rs only accepted http/https schemes — about:blank was rejected, causing silent failure on new session creation
Screencast relay exits when tx.send() finds 0 receivers; CDP sends the first frame before the UI subscriber can attach, causing a race condition
The browser tool injects sandbox context via SandboxRouter; the service has no such context and needed a default_sandbox flag
Tailwind classes used were already present — no rebuild needed for button styling changes

code.finding: Relay task exited on tx.send() with 0 receivers instead of dropping the frame and waiting — race condition when CDP sends first frame before subscriber attaches
obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) null-stripping added before serde deserialization; serde #[serde(default)] handles missing keys but not explicit null on u64 fields
Subscription filter silently drops events for clients that haven't subscribed to the event type
browser.screencast.frame was not in the subscription list so all screencast frames were silently dropped by the broadcast filter
validate_url rejected about:blank — needed special case for scheme 'about'
RealBrowserService::request() needed null-stripping (same as BrowserTool) since the UI also sends explicit nulls

workflow: Diagnostic logging at every stage of a relay chain (CDP → registry → subscriber → WS → JS) is effective for tracing frame delivery failures
Integration tests with #[ignore] annotation work well for tests requiring real browser containers
When shared state (BrowserManager) is needed across two services, Arc<OnceCell<T>> with a with_shared_manager() constructor is the idiomatic Rust pattern used here
Playwright e2e tests are placed in crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/ and extend base-test.js
YubiKey tap is required for git push — must be physically present during push operations

friction: Multiple 'Waiting for first frame' failures across different root causes (null crash, relay race condition, WebSocket subscription) made debugging iterative and slow
The screencast relay race condition required 3+ debug sessions to isolate — only found via added tracing logs
about:blank silently failing URL validation caused confusing UX where New Session appeared to do nothing
YubiKey requirement for git push interrupted flow multiple times
Pre-existing clippy error in moltis-voice crate caused noise during lint checks on unrelated crates
CUDA error appeared during workspace-wide clippy runs, unrelated to changes
open_items: Per-agent browser profiles not yet built — currently single global profile in host mode
Create Session button from UI is implemented but full manual-login cookie sharing with agent sessions (e.g. LinkedIn) not explicitly tested
Mouse delta_x/delta_y fields added to MouseInput type but Display impl for BrowserAction may not include them (referenced but not confirmed fixed)
Google suggestions in URL autocomplete rely on suggestqueries.google.com — CORS behavior in sandbox not verified
Session card click-to-select and visual highlight state committed but not user-verified
Playwright e2e tests written but not confirmed running in CI across all sandbox modes (apple-container, podman, docker)
